mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent recessionary pressures that compress corporate profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts reflects synchronized consumer demand across durable goods and real estate, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.03) when the macro regime shifts to monetary tightening or recessionary contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered periods, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, or the interaction effect shows negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 60-day equity returns in >60% of observations, or the relationship breaks down during financial stress periods (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity that triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when consumer demand proxies remain stable.
